description: OP issuer key-scoping policy. Enumerates each key in the issuer DID document and the credential types each key is governance-authorised to sign. Informational only; the protocol does not enforce these constraints cryptographically. Verifiers MAY consume this document to detect mis-scoped issuance. See AIP v0.8 §3.4.

legitimate_assertion_window.note: #key-1 was the active assertion key from genesis until 2026-05-21T20:33:22Z (the #key-2 activation). Credentials whose proof.created falls within that window were validly issued under this key. Genuinely archival ones are intentionally NOT re-issued; strict verifiers that ignore historical assertion windows will reject them, which is expected for archival artifacts and is NOT a revocation. Still-relied-upon artifacts (site-linked, AT Directory, BD collateral — e.g. the Cryptorefills merchant attestation) were re-issued under #key-5 at the 2026-06 keystate ceremony.

keys.notes: Retained in verificationMethod only — verifies historical credentials issued under this key. MUST NOT be used to sign any new credential under any circumstances. Issuance between 2026-05-21 and 2026-06-10 that referenced this key was mis-scoped (see ceremony 2026-06-keystate); affected credentials were re-issued under #key-5 (runtime) or #key-2 (offline org/principal). Historical-artifact handling: liveness split — live re-issued under #key-5, archival left as-is per the legitimate_assertion_window above. Custody correction: key-1 bytes were held in the service environment (contrary to the not_held claim published 2026-05-24) until the 2026-06-10 ceremony, when they were overwritten at the #key-5 cutover. This entry corrects the record.

Primary org root signing key. Lives only as an offline encrypted keyfile under the custodian's control. Signing is a deliberate manual offline action via the opkey CLI. NOTE: ObserverDelegationCredential and BitstringStatusListCredential issuance moved to #key-5 at the 2026-06-10 ceremony — runtime issuance cannot be served by an offline key.

Scoped on-server signing key for sub-100ms PolicyEvaluationCredential issuance. Held only on the policy evaluator server under restricted file permissions. Rotate independently of #key-2 if compromised.

Scoped on-server runtime issuance key (the #key-3 pattern, applied to delegation/status-list/receipt issuance). Generated on-server at the 2026-06 keystate ceremony; never leaves the server. The API performs a boot-time fail-closed check that OP_SIGNING_KEY derives to this publicKeyMultibase AND that this fragment is assertionMethod-listed — a doc/runtime desync now refuses to start instead of issuing mis-scoped credentials. Fragment #key-4 deliberately skipped: reserved for the AIP v0.9 World ID linkage provisioning.

AT Directory merchant-trust issuance key. Used at build time to sign MerchantTrustCredentials and the merchant-trust BitstringStatusListCredential. Type Multikey (eddsa-jcs-2022 proof suite). Private key held in ~/.at-keys/merchant-vc-key-6.hex on the build machine (mode 600). Not held on the API server — signing is a deliberate build-time action. Rotate on directory rebuild if compromised.
